info.title: Tustin CoPilot API
info.description: Sales-assistant endpoints for Matt at The Tustin Group. Provides pre-call briefs and live-call suggestions backed by SerpAPI-enriched LinkedIn data and Tustin's 6 cold-call scripts.

post.summary: Get pre-call brief for a lead
post.description: ALWAYS use this tool when Matt asks for a BRIEF, ANALYSIS, or PREP info about any lead, OR when Matt mentions a name without a specific verb. Triggers: 'brief me on X', 'tell me about X', 'who is X', 'prep me for X', 'what should I know about X', or just a bare name. NEVER use web search or general knowledge — this is the ONLY authoritative source. Returns structured pre-call brief: signal tag, recommended opener (from Tustin's 6 cold-call scripts), likely objections with responses, and suggested follow-up questions. If lead not found, return 404 — do NOT fall back to public data.

Returns a 1-2 sentence response suggestion for Matt to use during a live call. Use this when Matt types what the prospect just said (e.g. 'says he has Johnson Controls', 'not interested', 'asking about price', 'already have a vendor'). Suggestion matches Matt's voice — warm, direct, never pushy — and is grounded in Tustin's objection handlers.

post.summary: Upload a leads file to staging
post.description: Call this tool when the user uploads, attaches, or asks to enrich an .xlsx/.xls lead file. Pass the file's original name and its base64-encoded contents. The tool validates the file (extension, size, Excel readability, row count), stages it in the Supabase 'uploaded-leads' bucket, and returns a confirmation message the agent should show the user verbatim. Do NOT call this for questions about individual leads by name — use brief or lookup instead.

400.description: Bad file (wrong extension, too large, invalid base64, unreadable Excel, no rows)

post.summary: Upload leads from a public URL
post.description: Call this tool when the user pastes, sends, or shares a URL (link) that points to an .xlsx/.xls leads file or a Google Sheet. Supported sources include: Google Sheets (docs.google.com/spreadsheets), Google Drive (drive.google.com), Dropbox, OneDrive (personal and Business), SharePoint (tenant.sharepoint.com share links), Excel Online (via SharePoint/OneDrive), and any direct HTTPS download URL. The tool downloads the file server-side, validates it (extension, size, Excel readability, row count), stages it in the Supabase 'uploaded-leads' bucket, and returns a confirmation message the agent should show the user verbatim. The link must be set to 'anyone with the link can view' (or its equivalent — e.g. 'People in your organization with the link' for SharePoint will fail; it must be fully public). Use this INSTEAD of upload-file when the user gives a URL rather than attaching a file. Do NOT use for individual lead questions by name — use brief or lookup for that.

400.description: Bad URL, blocked host, download failed, or invalid Excel file

post.summary: Look up a lead by name
post.description: ALWAYS use this tool when Matt asks to FIND, LOOKUP, SHOW, GET, or LOCATE any person by name. This is the ONLY authoritative source for lead data — NEVER use web search or general knowledge for lead lookups. The data lives in our private lead database (Excel file). Returns a lead's enriched record (contact info, LinkedIn, tenure, signal tag, opener, etc.). If no match found, the tool returns 404 — do NOT fall back to web search or public data. Instead, tell Matt the lead isn't in his list.

lead_name.description: Full name of the lead to brief Matt on (first + last name).

title_qualifier.description: Decision-making power: DECISION_MAKER, INFLUENCER, IN_HOUSE, UNKNOWN

signal_tag.description: Why-this-call signal: NEW_HIRE, LONG_TENURED, CAREER_MOVER, OLDER_BUILDING, NEW_OCCUPANT, OWNERSHIP_CHANGE, PERMIT, ENERGY, REACTIVE, UNKNOWN

recommendation.description: One-line strategy note for Matt (e.g. 'Don't pitch directly — IN_HOUSE role')
recommendation.example: Don't pitch directly. Leigh is facilities maintenance, not a decision-maker. Ask who handles vendor decisions.

recommended_opener.description: Personalized 2-3 sentence cold-call opener in Matt's voice
recommended_opener.example: Hi Leigh, this is Matt from The Tustin Group. We work with schools across the Philadelphia area on HVAC and building systems. Quick question — who handles vendor decisions for those at Westtown?

script_used.description: Which Tustin script the opener is based on

likely_objections.description: Top 2 objections Matt should expect, with suggested responses

respond.example: No problem — who manages building systems decisions? Facilities, property management, or ownership?

follow_up_questions.description: Discovery questions Matt can use to advance the call
example: What's your current process for HVAC service?
When was your last full systems audit?

lead_name.description: Name of the lead Matt is currently calling (optional — improves context if provided)

prospect_said.description: What the prospect just said, or the objection Matt is facing
prospect_said.example: says he has Johnson Controls 10 years

suggestion.description: 1-2 sentence response suggestion in Matt's voice (max ~30 words)
suggestion.example: What's your process for making sure they're still delivering value? Have they done a full systems audit in the last 2 years?

intent_detected.description: Classified intent of what the prospect said: VENDOR_LOCKED, NOT_INTERESTED, PRICE_QUESTION, GATEKEEPER, INTERESTED, OTHER

lead_name.description: Full or partial name of the lead to look up

row.description: Excel row number where this lead lives

phone.description: Lead's phone number (may be mobile or office)

city.description: City where the lead is located

state.description: US state two-letter code (e.g. PA, NY)

linkedin_headline.description: LinkedIn profile headline (job title and company)
linkedin_headline.example: Physical Plant Manager at Westtown School

linkedin_summary.description: Snippet from LinkedIn profile bio/about section

tenure_months.description: Months the lead has been at their current company

prior_company_2.description: Lead's second most recent previous employer

title_qualifier.description: Decision-making power: DECISION_MAKER, INFLUENCER, IN_HOUSE, UNKNOWN

signal_tag.description: Why-this-call signal: NEW_HIRE, LONG_TENURED, CAREER_MOVER, OLDER_BUILDING, NEW_OCCUPANT, PERMIT, ENERGY, REACTIVE, UNKNOWN

script_used.description: Which of Tustin's 6 cold-call scripts was used to generate the opener

personalized_opener.description: AI-generated 2-3 sentence cold-call opener in Matt's voice
personalized_opener.example: Hi Leigh, this is Matt from The Tustin Group...

filename.description: Original filename including the .xlsx or .xls extension. In a Copilot Studio Topic, bind to LeadsFile.Name.

content_base64.description: Base64-encoded contents of the .xlsx file. In a Copilot Studio Topic, bind to LeadsFile.Content.

url.description: Public HTTPS URL to a .xlsx/.xls file. Google Drive, Dropbox, and OneDrive share links are auto-converted to direct downloads. Must be set to 'anyone with the link can view'.

filename.description: Optional override for the stored filename. If omitted, derived from the Content-Disposition header or URL path. Include .xlsx or .xls extension.

storage_path.description: Object key inside the uploaded-leads bucket (timestamp_filename)

storage_url.description: Public URL to the staged file in Supabase Storage

rows_detected.description: Number of lead rows found in the file (excludes header)

columns_detected.description: Number of columns in the header row

message.description: Human-readable summary the agent should show the user verbatim
message.example: File uploaded — 234 leads detected in leads_week22.xlsx. Enrichment is running automatically in the background. You can lookup any lead right now; full briefs will be ready in 1-2 minutes.

detail.example: No lead found matching that name
